# Feature: Exclusion Gates — Filter WS/SSE from HTTP-Only Contexts

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-011
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

WebSocket and SSE requests live in collections alongside HTTP requests but cannot be executed by the HTTP-centric runners (collection runner, flow executor, test suite executor). This feature adds exclusion gates — filtering logic in three UI entry-points — so that WS/SSE items never appear in contexts where they cannot be run. The end user sees only HTTP requests when building a collection run, adding a request to a flow, or adding a request to a test suite; WS/SSE items are silently excluded from those lists. This prevents confusing errors and sets a clean boundary between protocol-specific and protocol-agnostic features.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No new entities, attributes, or schema changes. This feature only adds filtering logic that reads the existing `protocol` discriminant on `AnyCollectionRequest` (defined in FEAT-001). The `isHttpRequest()` type guard from `packages/core/src/utils/requestTypeGuards.ts` is the sole predicate used.

## 3. Files

Files this feature modifies:

- `packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx` — Filter `flattenRequests()` to HTTP-only
- `packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowRequestSearch.tsx` — Filter `flattenCollections()` to HTTP-only
- `packages/core/src/components/testlab/TestSuiteEditor.tsx` — Filter `flattenCollectionRequests()` to HTTP-only

Test files this feature creates:

- `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RunCollectionModal.test.tsx` — Unit tests for exclusion in RunCollectionModal
- `packages/core/src/test/components/flow/FlowRequestSearch.test.tsx` — Unit tests for exclusion in FlowRequestSearch
- `packages/core/src/test/components/testlab/TestSuiteEditor.test.tsx` — Unit tests for exclusion in TestSuiteEditor AddItemDialog

## 4. Dependencies

Features that must exist before this feature:

- **FEAT-001 (Protocol Types & Core Data Model)** — Provides `AnyCollectionRequest`, `WsCollectionRequest`, `SseCollectionRequest`, and the `isHttpRequest()` / `isWsRequest()` / `isSseRequest()` type guards. Already complete.
- **FEAT-010 (Collection Persistence for WS/SSE)** — Ensures WS/SSE items are correctly persisted with `protocol` discriminant so the type guards work on loaded data. Already complete.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Filter WS/SSE requests from RunCollectionModal

- [x] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/components/common/RunCollectionModal.tsx`, modify the `flattenRequests()` helper function (line ~52) to skip non-HTTP requests. The current condition `if (isRequest(item) && item.request)` should additionally check `isHttpRequest(item.request)`. The `isHttpRequest` import already exists (line 6). After this change, WS and SSE collection items will never appear in the Run Collection modal's request list, request count, selection set, or run payload. No changes needed to `RunRequestCard`, `useCollectionRunner`, or `httpRequestExecutor` — they already assume HTTP requests.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Create `packages/core/src/test/components/common/RunCollectionModal.test.tsx` with the following test cases:
  - Renders HTTP requests from a mixed-protocol collection (HTTP + WS + SSE)
  - Does not render WS requests in the request list
  - Does not render SSE requests in the request list
  - Run button count reflects only HTTP requests (excludes WS/SSE)
  - Shows "No requests found" empty state when a collection contains only WS/SSE items
  - Legacy requests (no `protocol` field) are included as HTTP
  - Search filtering works on the HTTP-only subset
- [x] **Documentation Update**: Add a JSDoc comment on `flattenRequests()` documenting the HTTP-only filter and its rationale.
- **Verify**: Open a collection containing mixed HTTP + WS + SSE requests, click "Run Collection". Confirm only HTTP requests are listed. Run `npx vitest run packages/core/src/test/components/common/RunCollectionModal.test.tsx` — all tests pass.

### TASK-002: Filter WS/SSE requests from FlowRequestSearch

- [x] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/components/flow/FlowRequestSearch.tsx`, modify the `flattenCollections()` helper function (line ~65) to skip non-HTTP requests. Add `isHttpRequest` to the existing import from `../../utils/requestTypeGuards` (currently only imports `isWsRequest`). Change the condition `if (isRequest(item) && item.request)` to also check `isHttpRequest(item.request)`. After this change, only HTTP requests appear in the "Add Request to Flow" search modal. The `isWsRequest` import can be removed since it is no longer used in this file (the method badge fallback `isWsRequest(item.request) ? 'WS' : item.request.method` becomes unnecessary because only HTTP requests pass the filter, so `item.request.method` is always available).
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Create `packages/core/src/test/components/flow/FlowRequestSearch.test.tsx` with the following test cases:
  - Renders only HTTP requests from mixed-protocol collections
  - WS requests are excluded from the request list
  - SSE requests are excluded from the request list
  - Footer count reflects HTTP-only requests
  - Shows "No requests found in collections" when all requests are WS/SSE
  - Legacy requests (no `protocol` field) are included
  - Search query filters within the HTTP-only subset
  - Clicking a request calls `onSelectRequest` with HTTP request data
- [x] **Documentation Update**: Add a JSDoc comment on `flattenCollections()` documenting the HTTP-only filter.
- **Verify**: Open the Flow editor, click "Add Request". Confirm only HTTP requests appear. Run `npx vitest run packages/core/src/test/components/flow/FlowRequestSearch.test.tsx` — all tests pass.

### TASK-003: Filter WS/SSE requests from TestSuiteEditor AddItemDialog

- [x] **Implementation**: In `packages/core/src/components/testlab/TestSuiteEditor.tsx`, add HTTP-only filtering **only for AddItemDialog request sourcing**, not for general request flattening used by display lookups. The current `flattenCollectionRequests()` helper is reused both by AddItemDialog and by `TestItemRow` display resolution; filtering it globally would hide metadata for already-saved WS/SSE test items and degrade row rendering. Refactor to either (a) add a dedicated `flattenHttpCollectionRequests()` helper for AddItemDialog, or (b) add an explicit option flag (e.g., `{ httpOnly: true }`) used only in AddItemDialog. Use `isHttpRequest` from `../../utils/requestTypeGuards` as the gate predicate. Keep existing non-HTTP-safe lookup behavior for `TestItemRow` so legacy or pre-existing WS/SSE test items still render stable names/subtitles.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Create `packages/core/src/test/components/testlab/TestSuiteEditor.test.tsx` with the following test cases focused on the AddItemDialog:
  - Renders only HTTP requests in the Requests tab of AddItemDialog
  - WS requests are excluded from the Requests tab
  - SSE requests are excluded from the Requests tab
  - Requests tab count label reflects HTTP-only count
  - Shows "No requests available" when all collection requests are WS/SSE
  - Legacy requests (no `protocol` field) are included
  - Flows tab is unaffected (flows are always shown)
  - Selected request can be added to the test suite
  - Existing WS/SSE request test items already present in a suite still render their row fallback details (no regression from AddItemDialog filtering change)
- [x] **Documentation Update**: Add a JSDoc comment on `flattenCollectionRequests()` documenting the HTTP-only filter.
- **Verify**: Open a test suite, click "Add Items", switch to the Requests tab. Confirm only HTTP requests appear; flows tab is unaffected. Run `npx vitest run packages/core/src/test/components/testlab/TestSuiteEditor.test.tsx` — all tests pass.

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A — exclusion gates are pure UI filtering; they do not interact with authentication.
- **Error Handling**: No new error paths. The gates prevent non-HTTP requests from reaching HTTP-only executors, eliminating a category of potential runtime errors.
- **Logging**: N/A — no logging changes needed. Filtered items are silently excluded (not errors).
- **Performance**: Negligible impact. Each gate adds one `isHttpRequest()` call per collection item during flattening — a simple property check (`protocol === 'http' || protocol === undefined`).

## 7. State Tracking

- [x] TASK-001: Filter WS/SSE requests from RunCollectionModal
- [x] TASK-002: Filter WS/SSE requests from FlowRequestSearch
- [x] TASK-003: Filter WS/SSE requests from TestSuiteEditor AddItemDialog
